title: 30th International Kangaroo Mathematics Contest 2014 — Écolier (Grades 3-4)
disclaimer: Official Math Kangaroo past paper. For personal educational practice only.

questions.question_text: Which drawing is the central part of the picture with the star?
questions.solution: See PDF page 1 for the figure. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: Jacky wants to insert the digit 3 somewhere in the number 2014. Where should she insert the digit 3 if she wants her ﬁve-digit number to be as small as possible?
questions.solution: Insert digit 3 into 2014 to get the largest number. Try each position: 32014, 23014, 20314, 20134, 20143. Largest = 32014 (insert before the 2). Answer: D.
solution_steps: Insert 3 before the leading 2. Answer: D.

questions.question_text: Which houses are made using exactly the same pieces of triangular or rectangular shape?
questions.solution: See PDF page 1 for the figure. Correct answer: A.

questions.question_text: When Koko the Koala does not sleep, he eats 50 grams of leaves per hour. Yesterday, he slept 20 hours. How many grams of leaves did he eat yesterday?
questions.solution: See PDF page 1 for this question. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: Maria subtracts and gets as results the numbers from zero to ﬁve. She connects the dots, starting at the dot with the result 0 and ending at the dot with the result 5. Which ﬁgure does she get?        
questions.solution: See PDF page 1 for the figure. Correct answer: A.

questions.question_text: Adam built fewer sandcastles than Martin but more than Susan. Lucy built more sandcastles than Adam and more than Martin. Dana built more sandcastles than Martin but fewer than Lucy. 1
questions.solution: See PDF page 1 for this question. Correct answer: E.

questions.question_text: Monica writes numbers in the diagram so that each number is the product of the two numbers below it. Which number should she write in the grey cell? 64 1 1 2 2
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for the figure. Correct answer: E.

questions.question_text: Ann has four pieces as shown. With these pieces she can completely cover the shape. Where should she put the piece ?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for the figure. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: Mr. Brown has painted ﬂowers on the store window (see picture). How does these ﬂowers look like from the other side of the window? 2 KSF 2014 – ﬁnalized problems Ecolier
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for the figure. Correct answer: E.

questions.question_text: Some candies were in a bowl. Sally took half of the candies. Then Tom took half of the candies left in the bowl. After that Clara took half of the remaining candies. In the end there were 6 candies in the bowl. How many candies were in the bowl at the beginning?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for this question. Correct answer: E.

questions.question_text: Which tile must be added to the picture so that the total light grey area is as big as the total dark grey area? ?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for the figure. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: Paula shoots arrows at the following target. When she misses, she obtains zero points. Paula shoots two arrows and adds the number of points. Which of the following sums cannot be her score? 30 50 70
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for the figure. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: Mary had equal number of green, yellow, and red tokens. She used some of these tokens to make a pile. Y ou can see all used tokens in the ﬁgure. She still has ﬁve tokens which are not on thepile. How many yellow tokens did she have at the beginning? KSF 2014 – ﬁnalized problems Ecolier
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for the figure. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: Rabbit Borya likes cabbages and carrots very much. In a day he eats either 9 carrots, or 2 cabbages, or 1 cabbage and 4 carrots. During one week Borya has eaten 30 carrots. How many cabbages has he eaten during this week?
questions.solution: See PDF page 4 for this question. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: The solid in the picture was made by sticking eight equal cubes together. How does this solid look from above?
questions.solution: See PDF page 4 for the figure. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: How many dots are there in this picture?
questions.solution: See PDF page 4 for the figure. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: On Kangaroo planet each kangyear has 20 kangmonths and each kangmonth has 6 kangweeks. How many kangweeks are there in one quarter of a kangyear?
questions.solution: See PDF page 5 for this question. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: Seven children are standing in a circle. No two boys are standing next to each other. No three girls are standing next to each other. Which of these is true for the number of girls standing in the circle?
questions.solution: See PDF page 5 for this question. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: Eve arranged cards in a line as it is shown in the ﬁgure below. At each move Eve is allowed to interchange any two cards. What is the smallest number of moves Eve needs to get the word KANGAROO?
questions.solution: See PDF page 5 for the figure. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: A sequence of triangles of diamonds is made. The ﬁrst three stages are shown. In each stage a line of diamonds is added. In the bottom line the outer diamonds are white. All other diamonds in the triangle are black. How many black diamonds has the ﬁgure in stage 6? 1 2 3
questions.solution: See PDF page 5 for the figure. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: Kangaroo Hamish bought toys and gave the shop-assistant 150 Kangcoins. He received 20 Kangcoins back. Then he changed his mind and exchanged one of the toys for another. He got back an additional 5 Kangcoins. What toys did Hamish leave the store with? KSF 2014 – ﬁnalized problems Ecolier New diagram required.

options.E: the bus, the motorcycle and the tram

questions.solution: See PDF page 5 for the figure. Correct answer: A.

questions.question_text: Write each of the numbers 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 in the squares to make the addition correct. Which digit will be in the grey square?+
questions.solution: See PDF page 6 for this question. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: What is the largest number of small squares which can be shaded so that no square made of four shaded small squares appears on the ﬁgure?
questions.solution: See PDF page 6 for this question. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: Nick has written each of the numbers from 1 to 9 in the cells of the 3 × 3 table. Only four of these numbers can be seen in the ﬁgure. Nick has noticed that for the number 5 the sum of the numbers in the neighbouring cells equals 13 (neighbouring cells are cells sharing sides). He noticed the same applies for the number 6. Which number has Nick written in the shaded cell?
questions.solution: See PDF page 6 for the figure. Correct answer: D.
